examples.text:
أَنَا الَّذِي نَظَرَ الْأَعْمَى إِلَى أَدَبِي
وَأَسْمَعَتْ كَلِمَاتِي مَنْ بِهِ صَمَمُ
ʔanā allaḏī naẓara l-ʔaʕmā ʔilā ʔadabī
examples.english:
I am he whose set-down verse the blind have seen,
whose words and rhymes have echoed in unhearing ears!

etymology.text: An innovation in Arabic, formed from اَل (al-, “the”) + لَ (la, “truly”) + ذِي (ḏī, “this”). Compare Biblical Hebrew הלזה (hallāze, “this, that”).
